title: Adaptive point count scaling

tests.name: Desktop (high tier) uses ≥5× more points than mobile (low tier)
tests.description: Compute quality profiles for high-end desktop and low-end mobile signals, then calculate effective point counts using the scaling formula. Assert the desktop count is at least 5× the mobile count.
tests.approach: Call computeQuality with desktop signals (DPR=2, cores=16, memory=8, no touch, 2560×1440) and mobile signals (DPR=1, cores=2, memory=1, touch, 320×568). For each profile, create a pointCloud with that profile's maxPoints, init with density and structureComplexity derived from capabilityToDensity/profileToComplexity for the respective device. Compare getPointCount results.

tests.name: Mobile low-tier still maintains volumetric depth (Z spread > 0.5)
tests.description: Create a point cloud with low-tier maxPoints (200) and minimum density (0.3), verify the effective count is still enough points to form a 3D shape and that positions have meaningful Z-axis variance.
tests.approach: Create pointCloud with maxPoints=200, init with density=0.3 and structureComplexity=0.2 (worst case mobile). Read position buffer, compute Z range (max - min). Assert count >= 1 and Z spread > 0.5.

tests.name: Scaling affects geometry count, not dimensionality — all tiers produce 3-component positions with Z variance
tests.description: For each tier (low/medium/high), create a pointCloud with the tier's maxPoints, init with representative density. Verify point count increases with tier while all tiers produce 3D positions with positive Z variance.
tests.approach: Use computeQuality for low/medium/high device profiles. For each: create pointCloud, init, verify position attribute itemSize=3, verify Z std-dev > 0.1, collect point counts. Assert low < medium < high counts.
assertions: position attribute itemSize === 3 for all tiers
Z standard deviation > 0.1 for all tiers
all tiers produce non-zero point counts

tests.name: No 2D fallback on low tier — all four geometry systems produce THREE.Points with ShaderMaterial
tests.description: Create all four geometry systems (pointCloud, particleField, ribbonField, crystalField) using low-tier maxPoints. Assert each adds a THREE.Points mesh (not Mesh/Line) with ShaderMaterial to the scene.
tests.approach: For each system: create with low-tier config (maxPoints=200/maxParticles=150), init with defaultParams, find THREE.Points in scene.children, assert it exists, assert material is ShaderMaterial with non-empty vertexShader.
assertions: each system adds exactly one THREE.Points mesh to scene
no THREE.Mesh or THREE.Line children present

tests.name: Effective point count formula is consistent: floor(density × maxPoints × (0.6 + complexity × 0.4))
tests.description: For a set of known density/complexity/maxPoints inputs, verify each geometry system produces the expected effective count matching the formula.
tests.approach: Use fixed params (density=0.7, structureComplexity=0.8, maxPoints=500). Expected count = floor(0.7 * 500 * (0.6 + 0.8 * 0.4)) = floor(350 * 0.92) = 322. Create pointCloud, ribbonField, crystalField each with maxPoints=500 and verify getPointCount matches expected.
assertions: particleField count matches formula with its maxParticles

tests.name: Minimum point floor: density near zero still produces at least 1 point
tests.description: Call createPointCloud with very low density (0.01) and low complexity (0.2) and maxPoints=200. Verify result is at least 1 (current floor).
tests.approach: Create pointCloud with maxPoints=200, init with density=0.01, structureComplexity=0.2. Assert getPointCount >= 1.

tests.name: capabilityToDensity produces lower density for mobile vs desktop device profiles
tests.description: Verify the density mapping function produces significantly different values for mobile (DPR=1, cores=2) vs desktop (DPR=2, cores=16) inputs, ensuring the multiplier doesn't compress the tier gap.
tests.approach: Call mapSignalsToVisuals with mobile signals (DPR=1, cores=2) and desktop signals (DPR=2, cores=16). Compare density values. Assert desktop density >= mobile density × 1.5.

tests.name: Point count never exceeds maxPoints cap at maximum density and complexity
tests.description: For each geometry system, init with density=1.0 and structureComplexity=1.0 (maximum inputs). Verify effective count does not exceed the configured maxPoints.
tests.approach: For maxPoints values [200, 500, 1200], create pointCloud with each, init with density=1.0, structureComplexity=1.0. Assert getPointCount <= maxPoints for each.

tests.name: Low-tier point cloud positions are volumetric, not coplanar
tests.description: Create a point cloud with low-tier config. Verify X, Y, and Z all have non-trivial variance (std-dev > 0.1), confirming the cloud is a 3D volume not a flat plane.
tests.approach: Create pointCloud with maxPoints=200 (low tier), init with density=0.5. Compute standard deviation of X, Y, Z components from position buffer. Assert all three > 0.1.

tests.name: Quality tier maxPoints values maintain the expected ratio hierarchy
tests.description: Verify the quality profile maxPoints values across tiers maintain a clear hierarchy where high > medium > low for all point budget fields.
tests.approach: Call computeQuality for low/medium/high device signals. Assert maxPoints, maxParticles, and maxRibbonPoints are strictly increasing across tiers.

tests.name: No localStorage or cookie access during adaptive scaling operations
tests.description: Spy on Storage.prototype.getItem and document.cookie during the full flow: computeQuality → create all systems → init → draw. Assert no storage access occurs.
tests.approach: Set up spies before creating systems. Run computeQuality, create pointCloud and particleField with resulting profile, init and draw each. Assert neither spy was called.

testHelpers.makeSignals: Factory for BrowserSignals with sensible defaults and overrides
testHelpers.makeFrame: Factory for FrameState with sensible defaults and overrides
testHelpers.desktopSignals: BrowserSignals preset: DPR=2, cores=16, memory=8, no touch, 2560×1440
testHelpers.mobileSignals: BrowserSignals preset: DPR=1, cores=2, memory=1, touch, 320×568

imports: three (real, not mocked — geometry systems use it directly)
